questions.question: What is the correct command to install Claude Code globally?
questions.explanation: Claude Code is a Node.js package installed globally via npm: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code.

questions.question: Which authentication method is recommended for individual developers using Claude Code?
questions.explanation: The first-run OAuth flow automatically opens your browser for login and stores the token locally — no manual key management needed.

questions.question: Where does the global CLAUDE.md file live?
questions.explanation: The user-global CLAUDE.md lives at ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and applies to all projects.

questions.question: What does the `/init` slash command do?
options: Creates a new project from a template
Scans the project and generates a CLAUDE.md file
Resets Claude Code to factory settings
questions.explanation: The /init command scans your project's structure, tech stack, and conventions, then generates a CLAUDE.md file to help Claude understand your project.

questions.question: How do you check which version of Claude Code is installed?
questions.explanation: Running claude --version displays the installed version of Claude Code.

questions.question: What happens the first time you run `claude` after installation?
options: It immediately starts a coding session with no setup
It prompts you to create a CLAUDE.md file
It opens a browser for OAuth authentication
It runs a diagnostic test on your system
questions.explanation: On first run, Claude Code initiates an OAuth flow that opens your browser so you can sign in with your Anthropic account.
